definition: The eye as a sense faculty within the six sense fields.
notes: The project prefers eye as the default because the term usually names the faculty straightforwardly. Visual faculty is acceptable in technical explanatory prose where the sense-base role must be made explicit and distinguished from visible forms or the act of seeing.

context_rules.context: In most source-facing and doctrinal contexts
In analytical prose emphasizing the faculty-role within the six sense fields
context_rules.notes: Use when plain eye might blur faculty and organ.

example_phrases.pali: cakkhuñca paṭicca rūpe ca uppajjati cakkhuviññāṇaṃ
example_phrases.translation: dependent on the eye and forms, eye-knowing arises

example_phrases.notes: Shows the term inside āyatana analysis.
